District of Columbia · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology under the DC Department of Health (DOH), Health Regulation and Licensing Administration. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$187
$85 application + $102 license fee
Renewal
$164
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 8 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology

Mailing Address

899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002

Physical Address

899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002

Phone & Fax

Phone: (202) 724-8800

Fax: (202) 724-8677

Email

doh.hpla@dc.gov

Credential Categories

District of Columbia SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in the District of Columbia.

Supervised Practice Registration

For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision in DC.

Application Process

How to Apply for a District of Columbia Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

Submit Application to DC DOH

Submit application with fees to DC Health.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Background checkCriminal background check.
Telepractice

District of Columbia Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

DC permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. DC is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid DC SLP license is required to treat clients in the District.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

DC offers a Supervised Practice Registration for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

DC does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full DC licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

District of Columbia SLP FAQ

How much does a DC SLP license cost?

The total initial cost is approximately $187. Biennial renewal is $164.

Is DC part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

No. DC has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

Does DC allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. DC permits telepractice but requires a valid DC SLP license.
